immutableProvenance.whySyntheticArtifactIsNeeded: The live android/scripts/docker-gradle.sh worktree now includes unrelated Docker-lane hardening beyond the original GRADLE_OPTS inheritance fix, so truthful provenance must isolate the exact behavior slice being claimed instead of hashing the full broader script.

scopeStatement.truthfulClaim: This superseding artifact isolates only the GRADLE_OPTS inheritance fix: docker run receives a script-owned GRADLE_OPTS value that contains only the AAPT2 override, so hostile caller networking overrides are not inherited into the container.
reviewedBehavior: Host-side GRADLE_OPTS values such as -Djava.net.preferIPv4Stack=true are blocked from reaching the container because docker run passes its own GRADLE_OPTS assignment.
notClaimed: This artifact does not claim later Docker-lane hardening such as analytics seeding, Kotlin in-process enforcement, image fingerprint labels, or help-text changes are part of the original GRADLE_OPTS isolation fix.
This artifact does not pretend the current full android/scripts/docker-gradle.sh file is feature-scoped.

historyForAndroidScriptsDockerGradleSh: 429bac4 feat: establish android port mission baseline
gitEvidence.currentWorkspaceContext: The current workspace still contains broader Docker-lane hardening in android/scripts/docker-gradle.sh, android/scripts/docker-gradle.Dockerfile, and android/gradle.properties; those broader changes were intentionally excluded from the synthetic feature-scoped artifact.

verificationInterpretation.exactArtifactBoundary: The hostile-GRADLE_OPTS validators are executed on the current workspace script because that is the runnable lane under review; the synthetic artifact narrows the provenance claim to the specific docker run GRADLE_OPTS override block within that runnable script.
verificationInterpretation.reasonThisIsTruthful: The current script still exhibits the isolated behavior claimed by the synthetic artifact, and the artifact explicitly excludes unrelated hardening from the provenance claim rather than pretending the full script hash is feature-scoped.
